pattern: A single greedy left-to-right scan with one pointer per string suffices: advance through the target consuming the candidate's characters in order, and success is having consumed them all.
hints: You only need to match the candidate's characters in order; greedily take the earliest possible match in the target.
Keep a pointer into the candidate; walk the target once and advance that pointer whenever characters match.
If the candidate pointer reaches the end of the candidate, every character was matched in order, so return true.

edge_inputs.description: empty candidate is always a subsequence
needs a later occurrence to succeed

stress.description: build t from random letters, then derive s as a true subsequence about half the time
stress.note: small alphabet plus a real-subsequence branch yields a mix of true and false
